decks.notes: This deck focuses on overwhelming opponents quickly with low-cost creatures and efficient spells to control the game. The key strategy is to deploy cheap threats like Delver of Secrets and Cryptic Serpent early on, while disrupting the opponent's game plan with counterspells and bounce spells. The deck also includes card draw and graveyard manipulation to maintain card advantage and tempo. By applying pressure early and maintaining control over the game, Mono Blue Aggro aims to secure the win before the opponent can stabilize.

This deck focuses on controlling the game through a combination of counterspells, removal spells, and defensive creatures. The Gates package provides mana fixing and card draw, while Squadron Hawk and Sacred Cat provide early defense. The deck aims to stall the game until it can establish a dominant position and close out the game with powerful finishers like Guardian of the Guildpact. The inclusion of card draw and efficient removal allows the deck to adapt to various strategies and maintain control over the tempo of the game.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly overwhelm the opponent with a fast-paced aggressive strategy focused on cheap creatures and burn spells. By efficiently utilizing mana and card advantage, the deck aims to deal consistent damage and finish off the opponent before they can stabilize. With a low mana curve and powerful synergy between cards like Goblin Bushwhacker and Burning-Tree Emissary, this Red Deck Wins archetype aims to put pressure on the opponent from the very beginning of the game and maintain that pressure until victory is achieved.

The deck focuses on creating a defensive wall of creatures like Axebane Guardian, Overgrown Battlement, and Shield-Wall Sentinel to generate mana and protect key combo pieces. Using cards like Freed from the Real and Galvanic Alchemist, the deck aims to untap its walls repeatedly to generate infinite mana and combo off with cards like Tuktuk Rubblefort and Reaping the Graves. Lead the Stampede and Winding Way are used to dig for combo pieces, while Quirion Ranger provides additional utility. With the right setup, the deck can quickly assemble its combo and overwhelm the opponent.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on early aggression with efficient creatures like Glint Hawk and Seeker of the Way, supported by burn spells like Lightning Bolt and Skred to close out the game quickly. The deck also includes cards like Relic of Progenitus to disrupt graveyard strategies and utilizes land cards like Alpine Meadow and Kabira Crossroads for mana fixing and lifegain. With a mix of creature aggression and removal, this deck aims to control the board and deal fast damage to overwhelm opponents in a Pauper 1 vs 1 format.

This deck focuses on using cheap creatures and various land-untapping cards to quickly fill your graveyard with powerful creatures. Once you have enough creatures in your graveyard, you can use cards like Dread Return to bring back creatures like Lotleth Giant or Scrapwork Mutt, which have powerful abilities when they enter the battlefield again. By carefully managing your resources and timing your combos effectively, you can overwhelm your opponent with a powerful reanimation strategy.

This Jund Aggro deck focuses on sacrificing creatures and generating value through various sacrifice effects. It includes cards that allow you to sacrifice creatures for benefits, recur them from the graveyard, and disrupt your opponent's game plan with removal spells. The deck also includes a mix of creatures with efficient stats and abilities that can help you apply pressure on your opponent. By sacrificing creatures and utilizing various sacrifice effects, this deck aims to overwhelm your opponent with relentless aggression and resource management.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures and direct damage spells. By utilizing low-cost creatures like Burning-Tree Emissary and Goblin Bushwhacker, along with efficient removal spells like Lightning Bolt and Galvanic Blast, the deck aims to consistently apply pressure and finish off the opponent before they have a chance to stabilize. The inclusion of cards like Rally at the Hornburg and Reckless Impulse helps maintain card advantage and keep up the pressure. With a fast and powerful strategy, this deck aims to win the game quickly and decisively.

The main focus of this deck is to control the board by countering opponent's spells with cards like Counterspell and Spell Pierce, while also using removal spells like Skred and Lightning Bolt. The deck also includes creatures with disruptive abilities like Spellstutter Sprite and Ninja of the Deep Hours to maintain control of the game. Additionally, the deck utilizes card draw and manipulation spells like Brainstorm and Ponder to dig for key cards and ensure consistency. This UR Control deck aims to outlast opponents and ultimately dominate the game with its versatile control strategies.

This Pauper 1 vs 1 Control Gates deck focuses on utilizing Gates as a mana fixing strategy while controlling the opponent's board with removal spells like Lightning Bolt and Prismatic Strands. The deck also includes creatures like Gatecreeper Vine and Guardian of the Guildpact for defense. The goal is to out-value the opponent by generating card advantage through cards like Thraben Inspector and Outlaw Medic, while also having the ability to finish off the opponent with cards like Armadillo Cloak and Basilisk Gate. Overall, the deck aims to control the game and secure victory through strategic plays.

The main concept of this deck is to control the game by utilizing Golgari and Jund Garden cards to overwhelm the opponent. By playing cards like Crypt Rats, Defile, and Cast Down, as well as using resources like Ichor Wellspring and Nihil Spellbomb, the deck aims to maintain board presence and removal options. The inclusion of Khalni Garden and Lembas helps with generating tokens for additional defense. Additionally, cards like Eviscerator's Insight and Troll of Khazad-dûm provide additional advantages in combat, while Snuff Out and Reckoner's Bargain offer flexibility in dealing with threats.

This deck focuses on utilizing cheap and efficient creatures and spells to maintain board control and pressure opponents early in the game. By playing disruptive cards like Counterspell and Spellstutter Sprite, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while steadily building up a board presence through creatures like Faerie Miscreant and Ninja of the Deep Hours. With a low mana curve and a mix of aggressive creatures and control spells, the deck aims to overwhelm opponents quickly and efficiently.

The main focus of this deck is to control the game by using various spells to counter opponent's plays and remove threats from the board. The deck utilizes cards like Counterspell, Skred, Lightning Bolt, and Spell Pierce to disrupt opponents' strategies and maintain board control. Additionally, cards like Lórien Revealed and Murmuring Mystic provide additional utility and board presence. The deck also includes card draw and manipulation spells like Ponder, Brainstorm, and Preordain to ensure consistency in drawing key cards. Overall, the deck aims to outlast opponents and secure victory through control and resource management.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly overwhelm the opponent with low-cost creatures and disruptive spells, while also benefiting from card advantage through drawing. By utilizing cheap creatures like Brinebarrow Intruder and Faerie Seer, along with countermagic like Spellstutter Sprite and Counterspell, the deck aims to control the tempo of the game and chip away at the opponent's life total. The inclusion of cards like Snap and Ninja of the Deep Hours provides additional ways to generate card advantage and maintain pressure on the opponent.

The main idea of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent quickly with a combination of low-cost creatures and spells, while also maintaining control through counterspells and card filtering. By efficiently utilizing cards like Delver of Secrets and Cryptic Serpent, the deck aims to apply constant pressure on the opponent and disrupt their game plan with counterspells like Counterspell. The inclusion of card draw and selection spells like Brainstorm and Ponder also ensures that the deck maintains consistency and finds key cards when needed.

This Jund Aggro deck focuses on quick creature plays and removal spells to apply early pressure on the opponent. Utilizing a combination of efficient creatures like Gixian Infiltrator and combat tricks like Krark-Clan Shaman, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent before they can establish a strong board presence. With support from cards like Cleansing Wildfire and Drossforge Bridge for card advantage and ramp, this deck seeks to maintain a steady stream of threats while disrupting the opponent's game plan. Overall, the deck prioritizes aggression and disruption to secure a swift victory.
